reasoning: Thunderbolts is a 2025 Marvel film focusing on an ensemble of secondary antiheroes/villains. Box office predictions and industry buzz suggest a relatively lukewarm reception, with global gross projections around the $400 million to $580 million range and domestic gross widely predicted to fall into the $150-$250 million range. The highest-grossing film of the year typically earns over $500 million (and often $600M+ or even $1B+) domestically. In 2025, Thunderbolts faces massively anticipated heavyweights like 'Avatar: Fire and Ash', 'Zootopia 2', 'Superman', 'Jurassic World Rebirth', and 'Wicked Part Two'. Market probability earlier on Polymarket was sitting at 1% for Thunderbolts to be the highest grossing movie, accurately reflecting its status as a mid-tier blockbuster in an increasingly crowded and top-heavy release calendar. There is virtually no credible likelihood that Thunderbolts beats these behemoths to become the number one domestic release of 2025. Given the constraints of the platform, the assigned probability is the minimum allowable (0.02).
